description:
Implement the frozen architecture.md §10/§15 blocker-model revision (landed as design by bl-cac0 + the bl-7d46 coherence pass) that moved past the current code. NOT a config concern — orthogonal to the bl-4c54/bl-f74d config-primacy overhaul. Three coupled parts, one ball because each is meaningless without the others:

1. on = ANY op. Today src/task.rs has 'enum On { Claim, Close }'. Spec §10: 'Core stores the schema AND enforces every blocker (the .pre of the named on op rejects while unresolved).' §15: 'on is now ANY op (claim/close sugared).' Widen On to carry any verb.

2. Generalize enforcement (extends bl-295e, do not revert it). src/enforce.rs currently has two hardcoded entry points enforce::claim/enforce::close, called from change.rs Occupancy::stage / Retire::stage. The frozen model wants a single op-keyed guard: 'for op O, reject (PermissionDenied) if any unresolved blocker has on==O', evaluated at BaseChange::stage before the seal. claim/close keep their named ready()/closeable() predicate SPELLINGS (§10: 'claim refuses an unresolved claim-blocker (ready() false); close refuses an unresolved close-blocker (closeable() false)'). enforce::blocked() is already generic over (verb, on), so this is additive. bl-295e is explicitly ratified by the spec ('Enforcement is CORE', citing the bl-70f5 revision) — nothing to undo, only widen.

3. Containment ⊥ blocking front-door (change.rs::Create + src/mutate.rs). Spec §10: '--parent E -> child parent: E (CONTAINMENT only — gates nothing)'. Today Create/mutate auto-mint an On::Claim reciprocal on --parent (the 'load-bearing subtraction' the spec calls out: old --parent conflated lives-under with gates). Required: --parent gates nothing; add '--blocks OP' (E.blockers += {child, OP}; OP required, no default-gated transition; --blocks ID:OP gates a non-parent) and '--needs B[:OP]' (self.blockers += {B, OP}, default OP=claim); retire '--gates X' (= --parent X --blocks close). 'epic' stays emergent containment that gates nothing by itself.

Acceptance: a blocker with a third 'on' is enforced by core; --parent alone creates no blocker; --blocks/--needs spell explicit edges; --gates removed; ready()/closeable() unchanged; 100% coverage; all files <=299 lines. See docs/architecture.md §10 (lines ~541-622) and §15 open-topics bullet. No open ball owns this (bl-b465 open-topics epic is closed/archived; bl-bdeb/bl-35f7 are dispatch wiring).
title: Generalize the blocker model: on=any op + containment⊥blocking front-door (§10/§15 impl)
